name: Mahalakshmi
description: Monthly financial assistance of ₹2,500 to every woman, LPG cylinders at ₹500, and free travel for women in TSRTC buses
promised: ₹2,500/month to every woman head of household, LPG at ₹500, free bus travel for women
delivered: Free bus travel for women implemented (serving ~35 lakh women daily, ₹7,422 crore reimbursed to TSRTC). ₹500 gas cylinder subsidy launched Feb 27, 2024 for ~40 lakh white ration card families. ₹2,500/month cash transfer: zero disbursements as of March 2026. Application forms collected in Jan 2024 but no implementation steps initiated. Estimated annual cost: ~₹50,000 crore for ~90 lakh women.

name: Rythu Bharosa
description: ₹15,000/acre financial assistance to farmers, crop loan waiver up to ₹2 lakh, interest-free loans up to ₹3 lakh
promised: ₹15,000/acre/year to farmers and tenant farmers, ₹12,000/year to agri labour, crop loan waiver up to ₹2L, interest-free loans up to ₹3L
delivered: Rythu Bharosa launched Jan 26, 2025 at ₹12,000/acre/year (20% below promised ₹15,000). Day-one: ₹569 crore to 4,41,911 farmers. Government claims ₹9,000 crore reached 70 lakh farmers in 9 days. Crop loan waiver: ~₹20,617 crore disbursed to ~25 lakh farmers; target was 50 lakh (₹31,000 crore). ₹12,000 to agri labour: not started. 793 farmer suicides recorded Dec 2023-Nov 2025 (vs 178 in BRS's final year 2022).

name: Gruha Jyothi
description: Free electricity up to 200 units per household per month
promised: 200 units of free electricity to every household
delivered: Operational since February 2024. ~42.25 lakh households receiving zero bills. Issues: ~2.50 lakh eligible applicants in Hyderabad still receiving bills. Billing cycle inconsistencies push some households above the 200-unit threshold. Reports of declining beneficiary numbers suggest possible silent rollback.

name: Indiramma Indlu
description: House site for families without own house and ₹5 lakh financial assistance for construction
promised: Free house site + ₹5 lakh construction assistance to every homeless family
delivered: 4.5 lakh houses sanctioned with ₹5 lakh assistance each. First houses inaugurated on September 3, 2025 — 21 months after taking office. Full-scale construction has not begun in most districts. Budget allocation: ₹5,800 crore; ₹800 crore spent (14% utilization).

name: Yuva Vikasam
description: Fill 2 lakh government jobs in the first year, Vidya Bharosa cards for students, skill development
promised: 2,00,000 government jobs in first year, Vidya Bharosa education cards (₹5 lakh/student), free internet for students, ₹4,000/month unemployment allowance
delivered: ~57,946 posts filled by March 2025 (29% of target). Key: Group 1 (562), Group 4 (8,084), Mega DSC (10,006 teachers). SC sub-categorization froze new recruitment Oct 2024-Apr 2025. Vidya Bharosa cards: zero implementation. ₹4,000 unemployment allowance: zero implementation — Deputy CM publicly denied in Dec 2024 that Congress had made this promise. Youth unemployment at 20.1% (vs 14.6% national average).

name: Cheyutha
description: Monthly pension of ₹4,000 for senior citizens, widows, disabled, beedi workers, single women, and others
promised: ₹4,000/month pension to senior citizens, widows, disabled, beedi workers, single women, weavers, AIDS/Filaria patients, dialysis patients
delivered: Pension amounts increased from BRS-era ₹2,016 but conflicting reports on whether full ₹4,000 achieved universally. A February 2026 report describes the hike as 'slated to increase' in the upcoming budget — suggesting the full amount has not been reached. Coverage expanded but many eligible beneficiaries still excluded.

name: Free Bus Travel for Women
description: Free travel for all women on TSRTC buses statewide
promised: Free bus travel for all women on TSRTC services
delivered: Launched December 9, 2023 (day 2 in office). Serves ~35 lakh women daily. 222.50 crore cumulative free journeys. ₹7,422 crore reimbursed to TSRTC. The government's most visible and successful scheme.

name: Rajiv Arogyasri Expansion
description: Expand Arogyasri health insurance coverage from ₹5 lakh to ₹10 lakh
promised: Expand Rajiv Arogyasri coverage to ₹10 lakh per family
delivered: Coverage expanded to ₹10 lakh (from ₹5 lakh). 1,835 treatments covered across 1,410 empaneled hospitals. Benefiting ~90 lakh families.

name: Caste Census
promised: Comprehensive caste census of all households in Telangana
delivered: Completed November-December 2024. Covered 3.54 crore individuals across 1.12 crore households. Telangana became the first state to publish comprehensive caste data.

name: ₹500 Gas Cylinder
description: LPG gas cylinders at ₹500 for eligible families
promised: LPG gas cylinders at ₹500 for all eligible households
delivered: Launched February 27, 2024. Targets ~40 lakh families with white ration cards.

name: New Ration Cards
description: Issue new ration cards to eligible families excluded from PDS
promised: Ration cards for all excluded eligible families
delivered: ~8 lakh new ration cards issued since January 2025.

name: Crop Loan Waiver
description: Complete waiver of crop loans up to ₹2 lakh within 100 days
promised: Waive crop loans up to ₹2 lakh for all farmer families within 100 days of taking office
delivered: Disbursed ~₹20,617 crore to ~25 lakh farmers in four phases through November 2024. Target: ₹31,000 crore for ~50 lakh farmers — roughly 25 lakh farmers still waiting. Food security card requirement excluded many eligible farmers. Timeline: 100 days missed; took 11+ months.

name: 2 Lakh Government Jobs
description: Fill 2,00,000 government posts in the first year
promised: 2,00,000 government jobs to be filled in Year 1 (by December 2024)
delivered: ~57,946 posts filled by March 2025 (29%). Group 1: 562 posts. Group 4: 8,084. Mega DSC: 10,006 teachers. SC sub-categorization froze new recruitment Oct 2024-Apr 2025. Police (12,452 vacancies), Group 2, additional DSC, and forest beat officer recruitments remain pending.

name: ₹4,000 Unemployment Allowance
description: Monthly unemployment allowance of ₹4,000 to educated unemployed youth
promised: ₹4,000/month unemployment allowance to educated unemployed youth
delivered: Zero implementation. Deputy CM Bhatti Vikramarka publicly denied in December 2024 that Congress had made this promise, contradicting recorded statements by Priyanka Gandhi during the election campaign.

keyMetrics.label: Government Response
keyMetrics.actual: Deputy CM denied promise was made

name: Vidya Bharosa Cards
description: Education cards worth ₹5 lakh for students to cover educational expenses
promised: Vidya Bharosa cards worth ₹5 lakh per student for educational expenses
delivered: Zero implementation. No application process established. No pilot programme. No distribution.

name: Old Pension Scheme Restoration
description: Restore the Old Pension Scheme (OPS) for government employees
promised: Restore Old Pension Scheme for ~2.2 lakh CPS (Contributory Pension Scheme) employees
delivered: No Government Order issued. No committee formed. No timeline provided. Affects ~2.2 lakh government employees currently under CPS.

name: International Schools in Every Mandal
description: Establish Telangana International Schools in all 612 mandals
delivered: Promise quietly replaced by 'Young India Integrated Residential Schools' (YIIRS) — one per Assembly constituency (119 total, not 612). Foundation stones laid for 28 schools in October 2024. None operational as of March 2026.

name: Teacher Recruitment (Mega DSC)
description: Recruit 20,000 teachers through Mega DSC to fill school vacancies
delivered: 11,062 posts notified (Feb 2024). ~10,006 teachers received appointment letters by October 2024. 19,000+ teacher posts remain vacant. Second DSC notification (scheduled Feb 2025) not issued as of March 2026.
